_meta.note: Customer-facing strings for the SwiftUI installer GUI. Lifted from inline Text()/Button()/Label() literals per Rule 0.9 (strings extractable from day one). v1.0 ships English-only; v1.2 translation drops a parallel ViewCopy.{lang}.json next to this file. Interpolation uses Python-style {name} placeholders; the ViewCopy loader's string(for:fills:) helper substitutes them at render time. Do NOT add em-dashes – use ASCII '--' or en-dash with spaces.

device_registration_error.heading: We could not register this Mac

permissions_prewarm._meta: CX-14 Section E1 (2026-05-23) + CX-17 (2026-05-23): log-line strings emitted by PermissionsPrewarmer + customer-facing intro screen copy shown BEFORE the pre-warm fires. The intro screen names each of the four macOS TCC dialogs the customer is about to see, explains WHY each is needed, and offers Grant / Skip primary + secondary actions. The pre-warm then runs the four requests SERIALLY with an 800ms gap between each (CX-17 fix: the original concurrent burst fired all four popups in the same second and the customer missed two of them on Studio retest 2026-05-23). The log lines surface in the LogDrawer + lifecycle OSLog stream. Customer-facing copy in the macOS TCC dialog itself ('Ostler needs access to ...') lives in Info.plist's NS*UsageDescription strings, which macOS controls.
permissions_prewarm.starting: Pre-warming permission prompts: requesting Contacts, Calendar, Reminders, and Photos access up-front.
permissions_prewarm.contacts_denied: Contacts access not granted. You can change this later in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Contacts.
permissions_prewarm.calendar_denied: Calendar access not granted. You can change this later in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Calendars.
permissions_prewarm.reminders_denied: Reminders access not granted. You can change this later in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Reminders.
permissions_prewarm.photos_denied: Photos access not granted. You can change this later in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Photos.
permissions_prewarm.applescript_contacts_granted: Contacts automation access granted (allows the installer to read your contact card during setup).
permissions_prewarm.applescript_contacts_denied: Contacts automation access not granted. The installer will fall back to typing your details by hand. You can re-grant this later in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Automation.
permissions_prewarm.finished: Permission pre-warm complete. Continuing to install.
permissions_prewarm.intro_heading: Ostler needs four permissions to read your data on this Mac
permissions_prewarm.intro_subheading: macOS will show four dialogs, one after the other. Click Allow on each so the installer can complete without pausing later. You can change any of these later in System Settings.
permissions_prewarm.intro_contacts_reason: So the Hub can pre-fill names + addresses while you set up, and recognise who you talk to most.
permissions_prewarm.intro_calendar_reason: So the Hub can surface what is on next and link people to events on the wiki.
permissions_prewarm.intro_reminders_reason: So the Hub can push to-dos from emails, meetings, and chats into Apple Reminders.
permissions_prewarm.intro_photos_reason: So the Hub can read photo metadata (date, location, captions) and tie memories to people, places, and events.
permissions_prewarm.intro_privacy_note: Your data stays on this Mac. Nothing is uploaded.
permissions_prewarm.denial_summary_footer: You can grant these later in System Settings > Privacy & Security.

admin_access_required.heading: Administrator access required
admin_access_required.prompt_reason: Ostler needs administrator access to install required software and keep your Mac awake during setup.
admin_access_required.reassurance: Your password stays on this Mac, never sent anywhere. macOS will ask for it on the next screen.
admin_access_required.osascript_prompt: Ostler Installer needs administrator access to install required software and keep your Mac awake during setup.
admin_access_required.requesting_status: Requesting administrator access. A macOS password dialog will appear in a moment.

install_cancelled.heading: Installation cancelled
install_cancelled.body: No problem. Nothing was installed on your Mac and nothing was changed. You can run the installer again whenever you're ready.

install_failed_banner._meta: CX-14 Section E2 (2026-05-23): split into a minimal top banner (title only) and a rich body pane. The previous design crammed the heading + subtitle + four buttons into a single horizontal banner; the buttons wrapped on the 880pt window and the subtitle 'Something went wrong... Try again' read as a single throwaway sentence. The split surfaces the rich apology + next-step copy in the right pane (where the customer is already looking) while the top banner stays as a quiet always-visible failure marker. The 'Try again' button was dropped: terminating the app and asking the customer to re-launch is more reliable than an in-place restart, and the Quit option in the footer already does the same thing. support@ostler.ai is now hyperlinked inline via AttributedString rather than living in a separate 'Email support' label.
install_failed_banner.heading: Install failed
install_failed_banner.subtitle_default: The installer hit a fatal error and stopped.
install_failed_banner.body_heading: The installer hit a fatal error and stopped
install_failed_banner.body_paragraph_prefix: We are sorry for the interruption. The full installer output is below so you can see what happened. To get help, email
install_failed_banner.body_paragraph_suffix: (the Email support button below opens your mail app with a redacted log already attached), or copy the log yourself with the buttons below and paste it into a reply.
install_failed_banner.email_body_clipboard_instruction: My installer failed. The PII-redacted log has been copied to my clipboard. Please paste it below the line:
install_failed_banner.email_copied_hint: Log copied to clipboard – paste into the email

license_entry.heading: Your licence
license_entry.intro: Ostler needs a licence file before it can install. The licence arrived in your welcome email from support@ostler.ai. Save the attached file (it looks like `ostler-licence.json`), then drag it here – or paste the JSON contents below.
license_entry.paste_empty_error: Paste the licence JSON above first, or drop the file onto the box.
license_entry.footer_hint: Your licence stays on this Mac. It is verified locally with a key built into this installer – no network call is made during this step.
license_entry.read_file_error: Could not read the licence file at {filename}: {reason}
license_entry.drop_file_empty_error: The licence file at {filename} is empty (zero bytes). Re-download from your welcome email, then drop the fresh copy onto the box.
license_entry.signature_invalid_error: Signature check failed. This file is not a licence we signed, or it was edited after delivery.
license_entry.expired_error: Your licence expired on {expires_at}. Email support@ostler.ai to renew.
license_entry.short_id_guidance: That looks like a Licence ID, not the full licence. Please drag the licence file (ostler-licence.json) from your welcome email into the box above, or paste its full JSON contents here. If you cannot find the email, contact support@ostler.ai.
license_entry.drop_no_provider_error: Could not read the dropped item. Try again, or paste the licence JSON below instead.
license_entry.drop_url_resolve_error: Could not resolve the dropped file ({reason}). Try again, or paste the licence JSON below instead.
license_entry.drop_json_load_error: Could not read the dropped JSON ({reason}). Try again, or paste the licence JSON below instead.
license_entry.drop_text_load_error: Could not read the dropped text ({reason}). Try again, or paste the licence JSON below instead.
license_entry.drop_unsupported_type_error: That drop type is not supported (got {types}). Drag a .json file instead, or paste the licence JSON below.

device_limit.heading: This licence is on {count} Macs
device_limit.explanation: Each Ostler licence is valid on up to {max} Macs. This one is already at the limit, so we cannot register this Mac without freeing up a slot first.
device_limit.bullet_email_us: Email us at support@ostler.ai and we will free a slot for you. Quote your licence id below.
device_limit.bullet_buy_another: Or purchase an additional licence at ostler.ai if this is a new Mac you want to keep alongside your other ones.
device_limit.mailto_subject: Free up a slot for licence {license_id}
device_limit.mailto_body:
Hi Ostler team,

I have hit the device limit on my licence and would like to free up a slot so I can install on this Mac.

Licence id: {license_id}

Thanks.

fda_sheet.heading: Full Disk Access required
fda_sheet.reason_default: Ostler reads Safari, iMessage, Notes, Mail, Calendar, Photos and Reminders from local macOS databases.
fda_sheet.instructions: Open System Settings, find Full Disk Access in the Privacy & Security section, and tick OstlerInstaller. Then come back here and tap Continue.

fda_gate.heading: Welcome to Ostler
fda_gate.welcome: Thank you for buying Ostler. Setup takes around half an hour, a little longer on a first run while Ostler downloads its AI models, and it keeps filling in your world for a while after that. You can leave it running.
fda_gate.reads_lead: To work quietly for you, Ostler needs your permission to read a few things on this Mac:
fda_gate.privacy_line: Private by design. Everything stays on this Mac and is never uploaded.
fda_gate.permissions_note: Over the next few minutes macOS will ask you to approve these, plus a few system permissions Ostler needs to run. Just allow each one as it appears.
fda_gate.step_2: Switch on OstlerInstaller in the list.
fda_gate.step_3: macOS will ask you to quit. Quit and reopen, and setup carries on automatically.

onboarding_question.back_tooltip_default: Review previous answers. Editing is disabled in this version; cancel and re-run the installer to revise.
onboarding_question._meta_yes_no_per_prompt_overrides: CX-96 (DMG #48g+1, 2026-05-29): per-prompt-id overrides for the yes/no toggle's row label. The OnboardingQuestionView.yesLabel(_:) lookup tries onboarding_question.yes_label_per_prompt.<prompt_id> first and falls back to the generic onboarding_question.yes_label when absent. reuse_settings is the customer-facing 'use my previous answers' branch on the re-run path, where the generic Yes/No is too cryptic.

no_label_per_prompt.reuse_settings: Start over from the first question

onboarding_question.error_empty_text: Please enter a value to continue.
onboarding_question.error_empty_choice: Please pick one of the options.
onboarding_question.assistant_name_helper: The name in the field is a random suggestion – type over it with anything you like. Marvin, Samantha, Joshua, Friday, Athena, Sage, and Rosie are all popular choices.
onboarding_question._exempt_assistant_name_helper: assistant-name-exempt: F6.1 suggestion-pool exemplar, not a default

choice_label._meta_channel_choice: CX-18 (Studio retest #13, 2026-05-23): labels were out of sync with install.sh's semantics — install.sh accepts 1,2,3,4,5 with meanings iMessage / Email / iMessage+Email (recommended) / Skip / iMessage+Email+WhatsApp. The previous labels listed 1-7 (with 6 + 7 dead because install.sh rejects them via the choices=1,2,3,4,5 CSV) and mapped 3 to 'WhatsApp' which install.sh actually treats as 'iMessage + Email'. Founder report: 'Channels (Q7) still does not have the whole list of channels'. Labels now match install.sh case-block semantics 1:1. If install.sh's CHANNEL_CHOICE handler in install.sh ever grows new values, add the matching label here AND update the choices CSV passed to gui_read.

onboarding_question.fda_preset_recommended_subtitle: Most people start here. Includes Contacts, Calendar, Apple Mail, Messages, Safari history and bookmarks, Notes, Reminders, and your Documents folder. Sensitive sources like Photos face recognition stay off.
onboarding_question.fda_preset_everything_subtitle: Every data source Ostler supports. Adds Photos library (including face recognition), Mail attachments, Voice Memos, Apple Music listening history, Health app data, and Screen Time activity. Best for power users who want maximum personalisation.
onboarding_question.fda_preset_customise_subtitle: Pick each source individually. You can choose exactly which apps Ostler reads from, and turn anything on or off later in the Doctor section of the app.
onboarding_question._meta_consent_install_body: CX-18 (Studio retest #13, 2026-05-23): Q20 body was duplicating the headline ('Ready to install?' / 'Ready to install. By clicking...') AND rendered the type-INSTALL action as plain body copy AND rendered 'terms' as plain text. Split into five runs so the SwiftUI view can render INSTALL bold + terms as an underlined link, with no headline repetition. consentInstallBody() in OnboardingQuestionView.swift composes prefix + bold(install_token) + middle + link(terms_link_label) + suffix.
onboarding_question.consent_install_typed_mismatch: Type INSTALL exactly (case does not matter) to confirm, or click Cancel to go back.

assistant_name_suggestions.comma_separated: Marvin, Samantha, Joshua, Friday, Athena, Sage, Rosie
assistant_name_suggestions._exempt_suggestions: assistant-name-exempt: brand-warm pool of suggestion options shown to the customer at install time. Customer picks their own.

hint_panel._meta: Main content area copy. CX-14 D5 (2026-05-23): watchdog overlay shown when subprocess silence >= 15s without finishing -- pre-fix the watchdog only logged into the hidden-by-default LogDrawer so a wedged install looked identical to a slow one.

passkey_ack._meta: Q12 (passkey-ack) help-copy. v1.0 LAUNCH BLOCKER fix 2026-05-22 (DEFIB_2026-05-22_late_afternoon_passkey_fallback.md): Studio retests #10 + #11 confirmed Apple's framework excludes Apple Watch as user verification for Secure-Enclave-backed passkeys. Mac Studio (no Touch ID) literally cannot register an ASAuthorizationPlatformPublicKeyCredentialProvider passkey regardless of Watch state. v1.0 ships passphrase-primary: customer's typed passphrase IS the primary unlock, works on every Mac. Passkey convenience returns in v1.0.1 as a Touch-ID-only layer. All three variants collapsed to the same passphrase wording; modality branching kept for v1.0.1 reintroduction without a schema change.
passkey_ack.help_touch_id: Your knowledge graph is encrypted with a passphrase you choose on the next screen. You will type this passphrase each time you start the Hub UI. A separate recovery key is also generated and shown once at the end of the install. Press Continue when you're ready.
passkey_ack.help_optic_id: Your knowledge graph is encrypted with a passphrase you choose on the next screen. You will type this passphrase each time you start the Hub UI. A separate recovery key is also generated and shown once at the end of the install. Press Continue when you're ready.
passkey_ack.help_password_or_watch: Your knowledge graph is encrypted with a passphrase you choose on the next screen. You will type this passphrase each time you start the Hub UI. A separate recovery key is also generated and shown once at the end of the install. Press Continue when you're ready.

consent_third_party._meta: CX-18 (Studio retest #13, 2026-05-23): Q19 third-party-data acknowledgement was rendering as a wall of equal-weight body text with 'Legal note:' buried mid-paragraph and no visual separation between the customer-facing explanation and the GDPR fine-print. The SwiftUI view now overrides the default help-text render with two Text() views: a primary intro_body in standard body copy + a subordinated legal_note in smaller italic with reduced contrast. Bash-side MSG_PROMPT_CONSENT_THIRD_PARTY_HELP keeps the full text for TTY installs; the GUI uses these split strings instead. docs.ostler.ai/privacy/third-party-data link rendering is handled by the existing linkifyHelpText() Markdown post-processor.
consent_third_party.intro_body:
Any data you import from third parties (Google Takeout, Meta downloads, LinkedIn exports, etc.) stays on this Mac. Ostler stores it in your local knowledge graph; nothing leaves your device.

By continuing you understand and agree that you are solely responsible for the processing and keeping of this data on your machine, just like the email messages already on your hard drive.
consent_third_party.legal_note: Legal note: For records you import to this Mac, you are the data controller and processor under UK and EU law (UK GDPR Article 4(7) and 4(8)). Creative Machines never receives this data and is not the controller. Your processing for personal and household purposes falls within UK/EU GDPR Article 2(2)(c). Read more at docs.ostler.ai/privacy/third-party-data.

recovery_passphrase._meta: Q14 (recovery_passphrase) help-copy. v1.0 LAUNCH BLOCKER fix 2026-05-22 (sister to passkey_ack): the typed passphrase is now the SOLE primary unlock factor, not a fallback. All three variants collapsed to the same passphrase wording; modality branching kept for v1.0.1 reintroduction without a schema change.
recovery_passphrase.help_touch_id: This passphrase encrypts your knowledge graph and unlocks the Hub UI at each start. At least 12 characters. We cannot recover it for you. Storing it in a password manager is recommended.
recovery_passphrase.help_optic_id: This passphrase encrypts your knowledge graph and unlocks the Hub UI at each start. At least 12 characters. We cannot recover it for you. Storing it in a password manager is recommended.
recovery_passphrase.help_password_or_watch: This passphrase encrypts your knowledge graph and unlocks the Hub UI at each start. At least 12 characters. We cannot recover it for you. Storing it in a password manager is recommended.

recovery_key_reveal._meta: CX-53 (DMG ship, 2026-05-24): recovery-key reveal sheet rendered over InstallCompleteView when install.sh emits the RECOVERY_KEY marker. Mirrors install.sh.strings.en-GB.sh MSG_INFO_RECOVERY_KEY_REVEALED_* keys so the GUI + TTY paths read from a single editorial source.
recovery_key_reveal.heading: Your recovery key
recovery_key_reveal.body: Write this down or print it now. It's the only way back in if you lose your passphrase AND your Keychain becomes inaccessible. Ostler cannot recover it for you – the key never leaves this Mac and is not stored on any server.

pair_iphone._meta: CX-56 (DMG ship, 2026-05-24): iOS Companion pairing QR section rendered between the service tick list and the CTA buttons on InstallCompleteView. The Hub gateway exposes POST http://localhost:8000/admin/paircode (no auth needed on localhost) which returns a §3.3 envelope JSON; we render that JSON string as a 256x256 QR with an oxblood border. CM031 iOS app scans the QR + decodes the envelope.
pair_iphone.title: Already installed the app?
pair_iphone.help: Already have the Ostler app on your iPhone? Scan this code to pair it with this Mac. You can also pair later from inside the app.
pair_iphone.fetch_failed: Couldn't reach the Ostler gateway yet. It might still be starting up – click Refresh to try again.

get_ios_app._meta: #599 (2026-06-02): success-screen 'Get Ostler on your iPhone' section. Static QR + link to https://ostler.ai/ios, a stable redirect Andy repoints to the App Store listing once it is live, so the DMG never needs recutting for the store URL. This is the primary iPhone call to action; the pair_iphone QR is demoted below it.
get_ios_app.title: Get Ostler on your iPhone
get_ios_app.body: Scan the code to download the Ostler app, then open it to reach your wiki and your assistant from anywhere.

install_complete._meta: #599 (2026-06-02): completion screen reworked. Hero 'Ostler is installed' (calm, not 'everything is ready') -> a 'what's happening now' hydration explainer that is the focus -> 'Get Ostler on your iPhone' (get_ios_app) -> demoted pairing QR (pair_iphone) -> the per-service tick list moved into a collapsed 'Hub status' disclosure. CTAs sit in the sticky footer. All copy via Rule 0.9. Qualitative hydration timing only (no hard hours number).
install_complete.heading: Ostler is installed
install_complete.subheading: Ostler is set up on this Mac.
install_complete.hydration_body: Ostler is now building your private knowledge base in the background. The first pass can take a few hours, and your wiki and assistant get richer as it keeps working. You can close this window. It carries on by itself.

tailscale_connect._meta: CX-81 Tailscale step (2026-05-26): dedicated full-screen 'Connect your iPhone and Watch' view rendered by TailscaleConnectView.swift, dispatched from OnboardingQuestionView when prompt.id == 'tailscale_connect'. Pairs with install.sh §3.15 STEP_BEGIN of the same id. Two big buttons (setup / skip) plus a collapsible mini-FAQ.
tailscale_connect.heading: Connect your iPhone and Watch
tailscale_connect.body:
Tailscale gives this Mac a stable private address that your iPhone and Watch can reach from anywhere – encrypted, with no public exposure, free for personal use.

Without it, the Ostler app on your iPhone can only reach this Mac when you're on home Wi-Fi.
tailscale_connect.setup_button_sub: Takes about a minute – we'll open Tailscale and wait while you sign in.
tailscale_connect.skip_button_sub: You can come back to this from Settings any time.
tailscale_connect.faq_what_a: Tailscale is a free service that creates a small private network between your own devices. Your iPhone, Watch and this Mac get private addresses that only they can use. Nothing is exposed to the public internet.
tailscale_connect.faq_secure_a: Yes. All traffic between your devices is end-to-end encrypted with WireGuard. Tailscale itself can never read your data – it only helps your devices find each other.
tailscale_connect.faq_later_a: Yes. You can run the installer again with --repair, or open Settings inside Ostler and turn Tailscale on then.

confirm_learned._meta: End-of-install confirmation step (2026-07): dedicated 'Confirm what we learned' accept/correct screen rendered by ConfirmLearnedView.swift, dispatched from OnboardingQuestionView for prompt ids calendar_owner / calendar_type / identity_collapse / identity_namesake. Pairs with install.sh's 'End-of-install confirmation' block (lib/ostler-confirm-calendars.py + lib/ostler-confirm-identity.py). Prompt titles + evidence arrive already localised from install.sh.strings.{lang}.sh; this section carries only the GUI-side framing + card labels. Wire values on the cards ('y'/'n', 'different'/'me', calendar types) are pinned by tests/test_confirm_ui_gui_answer_contract.sh - do not translate values, only labels.
confirm_learned.calendar_owner_hint: Press Continue to accept this name, or type the right one.
confirm_learned.calendar_type_work_subtitle: Meetings and events from your work life.
confirm_learned.calendar_type_family_subtitle: Shared family plans – school, trips, birthdays.
confirm_learned.calendar_type_shared_subtitle: A calendar someone else shares with you – their events, not yours.
confirm_learned.calendar_type_other_subtitle: None of the other types fits this calendar.
confirm_learned.identity_collapse_y_subtitle: Combine them into one profile the next time Ostler updates itself.
confirm_learned.identity_collapse_n_subtitle: Nothing is combined. You can change this later.
confirm_learned.identity_namesake_different_title: Someone else who shares my name
confirm_learned.identity_namesake_different_subtitle: Keep us permanently separate – Ostler will never combine us.
confirm_learned.identity_namesake_me_subtitle: Leave it as it is for now – nothing is changed.
